questions.question: What is the effect of defining __slots__ in a class?
choices: It makes all attributes private
It restricts instances to only the named attributes, preventing a __dict__ from being created and saving memory
It speeds up method calls by caching them
It automatically generates __init__ for the listed attributes
questions.explanation: __slots__ is a class-level sequence of attribute names. When defined, Python does not create a per-instance __dict__, saving memory. Instances can only have the attributes listed in __slots__; assigning other attributes raises an AttributeError.

questions.question: The ________ decorator allows you to define a method that is accessed like an attribute, without calling it with parentheses.
questions.explanation: @property turns a method into a descriptor that is accessed like an attribute. When you access obj.x, the property's getter method is called automatically. You can also define setter and deleter methods using @x.setter and @x.deleter.

questions.question: What does the super() function do in Python 3?
choices: It creates a new superclass dynamically
It returns a proxy object that delegates method calls to the next class in the MRO
It prevents a class from being subclassed
It calls all parent class methods simultaneously
questions.explanation: super() returns a proxy object that delegates method calls to the next class in the MRO (method resolution order). In Python 3, super() can be called with no arguments inside a method, and it automatically determines the class and instance.

questions.question: What is a class decorator in Python?
choices: A decorator that can only be applied inside a class body
A function or callable that takes a class as an argument and returns a modified or new class
A special method that decorates instance attributes
A syntax for defining private methods
questions.explanation: A class decorator is applied with @decorator syntax above a class definition. It receives the class object as its argument and typically returns the same class (possibly modified) or a wrapper. Class decorators run after the class body has been executed.

questions.question: Python 3 computes the method resolution order using an algorithm called C3 ________.
questions.explanation: The C3 linearization algorithm computes a deterministic, consistent MRO for classes with multiple inheritance. It ensures that each class appears before its parents and that the local precedence order specified in class definitions is preserved.

questions.question: When extending a built-in type like list or dict by subclassing, what should you be aware of?
choices: Built-in types cannot be subclassed in Python 3
You may need to override multiple methods to fully customize behavior, and some internal operations may not call your overridden methods
Subclassing built-in types automatically copies all data
You must always use __slots__ when subclassing built-in types
questions.explanation: Built-in types can be subclassed, but their C-level implementations may bypass your Python-level method overrides for internal operations. For more predictable behavior, you can sometimes subclass from collections module classes (like UserDict, UserList) instead.

questions.question: What happens if a class defines __slots__ but one of its parent classes does not?
choices: A TypeError is raised at class creation time
The subclass still has a __dict__ because the parent without __slots__ provides one
__slots__ in the subclass overrides the parent and removes __dict__
The parent class is automatically updated to include __slots__
questions.explanation: If any class in the hierarchy does not define __slots__, instances will still have a __dict__ (inherited from that class). For __slots__ to fully prevent __dict__ creation, every class in the chain must define __slots__.
